steps.title: Identify the structural relationship
steps.content: The main clause ends with "it was not universally ______" and what follows immediately explains what "not universal" means: a "whereas" clause showing a divided response — some people embraced the balloon, others dismissed it. The blank must close the main clause and then introduce that explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'embraced:': A colon after "embraced" signals that what follows elaborates on the preceding claim. The "whereas" clause that follows directly explains how the embrace was divided — some flocked to launches, others dismissed the balloon — proving it was not universal. ✓
  • Choice B — 'embraced': No punctuation leaves "embraced whereas many flocked…" as a run-on; the "whereas" clause cannot attach grammatically without a separator. ✗
  • Choice C — 'embraced,': A comma is too weak to introduce an elaborating clause here; it creates comma-splice ambiguity and does not signal that what follows explains "not universally embraced." ✗
  • Choice D — 'embraced and': "And" cannot pair with "whereas" — "embraced and whereas…" is ungrammatical. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
